mechanism_narrative: Higher industrial production indicates stronger supply-side growth, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 20-day returns in periods of rising INDPRO are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent monthly inflation above 220 (CPIAUCSL) signals central bank inaction, eroding real returns and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 20-day returns in high-inflation periods exceed 1.5% on average or Rank IC > 0.05 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in federal funds rate and inflation (FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL > 225) signal tight monetary policy, reducing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 20-day returns in co-occurring high-rate/high-inflation periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC > 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates during a tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS increase > 0.2% in 1 month) signal reduced liquidity, depressing equity prices and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 20-day returns in rate-hike phases exceed 1.5% on average or Rank IC > 0.05 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly job gains (PAYEMS > 150k) indicate robust labor market conditions, boosting consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns in high-job-growth periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate exceeding 8.5% (UNRATE) signals slack in the labor market, reducing corporate profit margins and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 20-day returns in high-unemployment periods exceed 1.0% on average or Rank IC > 0.04 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts (HOUST > 600k) and real sales (RSAFS > 390k) indicates strong consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 60-day returns in high-demand periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth (GDPC1 > 3%) during late-cycle phases signals overheating, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days due to policy tightening expectations.
failure_condition: SPX forward 60-day returns in high-GDP growth periods exceed 2.0% on average or Rank IC > 0.05 over any 2-year window.
